conceptClusters.title: Retrieved path syntax and headings
exitEvidence: Learner resolves references from the exact document base, authors unambiguous attributes, and preserves a content-derived heading hierarchy before adding navigation.
Learner repairs a moved destination, malformed href, and broken heading outline without copying an earlier project structure.

conceptClusters.title: Hyperlink destinations and activation
exitEvidence: Learner creates genuine href-bearing hyperlinks for web, file, email, telephone, download, and fragment destinations and predicts their resolved URLs and activation behavior.
Learner distinguishes navigation from non-navigation actions, verifies keyboard and pointer activation, and never treats an anchor without href as an equivalent hyperlink.

conceptClusters.title: Purposeful text and fragment targets
exitEvidence: Learner writes link text whose destination and consequence remain understandable in programmatic context and in a links-only inspection.
Learner creates unique stable fragment targets and verifies URL state, target identity, heading context, visibility, focus state, and keyboard continuation separately.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked complete document gains a real hyperlink, a purposeful link-list entry, and a stable fragment destination with separate URL, DOM, activation, and accessibility evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A complete document, portable destination fixture, heading outline, URL resolver, hyperlink inspector, links-only view, and fragment trace are synchronized.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner explains four contrasts without equating anchors with hyperlinks, source strings with resolved destinations, nearby prose with reliable purpose, or scrolling with focus.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts the prediction with parsed attributes, resolved URLs, activation traces, link-list output, fragment state, heading context, target visibility, and focus evidence.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one evidence layer at a time after the learner commits a destination, purpose, or fragment prediction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss replays a smaller changed destination and requires the learner to identify the earliest wrong assumption before continuing.

interactions.title: Retrieve the link foundation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the resolved URL, parsed href value, and heading destination for a relative reference before inspecting source and document base evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A foundation prediction is committed beside the document.

evidence.observable: Source value, parsed value, document base, resolved URL, and destination heading agree.
evidence.changedCase: The current document nests one directory deeper.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Project root or visible heading text is used as the URL base.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve from the exact document URL first.

interactions.title: Inspect hyperlink activation
interactions.learnerAction: Compare an anchor with href, an anchor without href, and a button-like action using role, focusability, resolved destination, keyboard activation, and navigation evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A three-case hyperlink behavior trace is saved.

evidence.observable: Only the genuine hyperlink exposes and activates the intended navigation contract.
evidence.changedCase: The href changes from web navigation to a sandboxed mailto handoff.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tag name or pointer click alone defines a hyperlink.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect href, role, focus, activation, and outcome.

interactions.title: Predict purpose outside prose
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which of three link names remains understandable in a links-only view and which programmatic context can validly contribute to purpose.
interactions.artifactChange: A link-purpose comparison is committed.

evidence.observable: Link text, programmatic context, destination, and consequence support an unambiguous choice.
evidence.changedCase: Two links share text but lead to different destinations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual proximity or title attribute is assumed sufficient.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect link list and programmatic context.

interactions.title: Explain fragment navigation layers
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how source href, unique id, resolved URL fragment, target lookup, scroll position, heading context, target visibility, focus state, and next keyboard step relate.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete fragment-navigation causal map is saved.

evidence.observable: Every layer has a distinct input, observable, and failure boundary.
evidence.changedCase: A fixed obstruction and duplicated target identifier are introduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scrolling, target identity, visibility, and focus are conflated.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace URL, target, viewport, and focus separately.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A regional trail guide gains portable page, web, file, email, telephone, download, and fragment navigation with purposeful names and executable changed-case evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A complete multipage trail document set, destination manifest, sandboxed protocol and download fixtures, heading outline, and broken navigation requirements are supplied.
activityDesigns.completionState: The saved and reloaded guide passes URL, attribute, heading, hyperlink, purpose, links-only, fragment, visibility, keyboard, and relocation checks.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the failed destination, activation, purpose, target, context, visibility, or focus observable without writing the final href or link text.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Guided hints move from the failing evidence layer to a smaller parallel trail destination and only then to a syntax boundary.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires prediction, minimal repair, activation or audit evidence, and a changed trail case before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Resolve trail document paths
interactions.learnerAction: Predict relative, parent, root-relative, absolute, query, and fragment targets from the directory and nested trail document bases.
interactions.artifactChange: Six destination predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Every source reference resolves from the correct document or public base.
evidence.changedCase: The nested trail page moves another directory deeper.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All references are resolved from project root.
interactions.correctionRoute: Start from each referencing document URL.

interactions.title: Author href attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Add unambiguous quoted href and related link attributes to supplied anchors while preserving owner, names, values, separators, and duplicate-name rules.
interactions.artifactChange: Trail anchors gain valid parsed attributes.

evidence.observable: Parsed owner-value maps match every intended destination contract.
evidence.changedCase: A query value gains spaces and ampersands.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source appearance is accepted without parsed values.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize the first failing start tag.

interactions.title: Inspect trail heading destinations
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect page and section heading ranks, stable target locations, links-only labels, and the outline needed to orient a visitor after navigation.
interactions.artifactChange: The heading-target map is annotated.

evidence.observable: Every destination retains a coherent hierarchy and descriptive heading with styling removed.
evidence.changedCase: A new detour section nests under an existing route.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Heading rank follows visual size or fragment need.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild hierarchy from content relationships.

interactions.title: Predict real hyperlink behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Predict role, focusability, URL exposure, Enter activation, pointer activation, and navigation for href, missing-href, and action-like anchor cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Three behavior predictions are locked.

evidence.observable: Only genuine destination-bearing links meet the navigation contract.
evidence.changedCase: One anchor receives href while another becomes a non-navigation action.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pointer click or a tag name substitutes for behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect semantics and both input paths.

interactions.title: Connect local trail pages
interactions.learnerAction: Author directory-to-trail, trail-to-directory, sibling-trail, and nested-section hyperlinks using portable references and intended same-context navigation.
interactions.artifactChange: Four local page relationships become active.

evidence.observable: Resolved URLs, responses, destination titles, history entries, and keyboard activation all pass.
evidence.changedCase: A trail page and its section identifier are renamed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Resolved source string is accepted without activation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Follow URL through destination response and title.

interactions.title: Connect public web resources
interactions.learnerAction: Author absolute HTTPS links to versioned fictional park notices while preserving query and fragment components and avoiding unnecessary new browsing contexts.
interactions.artifactChange: Public notice destinations join the guide.

evidence.observable: Resolved origins, paths, queries, fragments, context behavior, and destination labels match the manifest.
evidence.changedCase: Public origin and query version both change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Displayed URL text or forced new tab replaces evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed destination and context outcome.

interactions.title: Test email and telephone handoffs
interactions.learnerAction: Build mailto and tel destinations, percent-encode controlled values, and test parsed schemes plus sandboxed handoff intent without sending messages or placing calls.
interactions.artifactChange: Safe contact handoff fixtures become inspectable.

evidence.observable: Parsed scheme, address or number, encoded fields, accessible name, and simulated handoff intent pass.
evidence.changedCase: Subject text and international telephone format change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: External side effect or string presence is used as proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect URL record and sandboxed handoff.

interactions.title: Test guide file downloads
interactions.learnerAction: Link a supplied trail PDF, declare the intended file name where appropriate, and verify resolved request, content identity, user choice, and bounded download behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: A deterministic guide-download path is added.

evidence.observable: URL, response, file identity, attribute, and sandboxed browser outcome remain distinct.
evidence.changedCase: The file moves and the response file name changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Download attribute is treated as an unconditional guarantee.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect request, response, preference, and outcome.

interactions.title: Predict link purpose
interactions.learnerAction: Choose destination-specific text for trail detail, closure notice, email, telephone, and PDF links before viewing the links-only inspection.
interactions.artifactChange: Five purpose predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Each name communicates destination or consequence alone or with valid programmatic context.
evidence.changedCase: Two resources share format but serve different purposes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Click here or raw URLs rely on surrounding appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read names in the links-only view.

interactions.title: Write trail link names
interactions.learnerAction: Replace repeated vague labels with concise purposeful text and make same-purpose destinations consistent while keeping different destinations distinguishable.
interactions.artifactChange: The guide gains a useful link-list vocabulary.

evidence.observable: Links-only, paragraph-context, destination, and consequence checks pass without redundant noise.
evidence.changedCase: A link changes destination while retaining old text and is rejected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Title attributes or hidden verbosity patch vague text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the destination and useful consequence directly.

interactions.title: Create unique fragment targets
interactions.learnerAction: Assign stable unique identifiers to existing trail headings and connect a contents list without changing heading ranks for styling or navigation convenience.
interactions.artifactChange: A heading-based fragment graph becomes active.

evidence.observable: Every href fragment resolves to exactly one intended heading target.
evidence.changedCase: A heading moves under a new parent and its target name changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible heading text or duplicate id is used as target identity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed id cardinality and hierarchy.

interactions.title: Test fragment arrival context
interactions.learnerAction: Activate each contents link by keyboard and pointer, then record URL fragment, target identity, scroll position, heading context, visibility, focus state, and next tab stop.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete fragment arrival matrix is saved.

evidence.observable: Navigation, scrolling, target visibility, focus, and keyboard continuation are verified separately.
evidence.changedCase: A fixed banner partially obscures one target and must be detected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Viewport movement alone is accepted as accessible arrival.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect each arrival observable separately.

interactions.title: Test activation input parity
interactions.learnerAction: Traverse all trail links with keyboard and pointer while verifying focus visibility, Enter activation, no focus-triggered navigation, destination, and history behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: An activation parity trace joins the project.

evidence.observable: Every genuine link is operable and predictable across input paths without activation on focus.
evidence.changedCase: One link loses href and another navigates on focus.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pointer success is treated as complete operability.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace focus and activation events separately.

interactions.title: Audit links without surrounding prose
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the complete links-only list, duplicate names, destination pairs, file types, contact consequences, and fragment purposes against the trail requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: A full link-purpose audit is annotated.

evidence.observable: Each link remains distinguishable with only programmatically determined context.
evidence.changedCase: Two formerly equivalent labels now lead to different trail states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual card or nearby paragraph supplies hidden context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate the programmatic context boundary.

interactions.title: Repair a moved trail destination
interactions.learnerAction: Move a nested trail page and PDF, rename one section target, and repair all affected href attributes, link names, and arrival evidence without machine paths.
interactions.artifactChange: Relocated content regains complete navigation.

evidence.observable: All affected references update, unaffected destinations remain stable, and old paths disappear.
evidence.changedCase: Move directions reverse and an absolute destination changes origin.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Only the first visible broken link is repaired.
interactions.correctionRoute: Search old identities and recompute every consumer.

interactions.title: Defend the trail link system
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and explain path bases, attributes, headings, destination types, activation, purpose context, fragment targets, visibility, focus state, and changed cases.
interactions.artifactChange: The guided trail navigation checkpoint is stored.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifacts and traces demonstrate all five concepts across destination types.
evidence.changedCase: A new trail notice and fragment are added without copying an earlier link.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A working click is offered without purpose or arrival evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen the weakest destination or purpose observable.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A partially scaffolded museum visit guide becomes a portable link system for galleries, tickets, access information, contacts, files, and exhibit fragments.
activityDesigns.startingState: Complete museum documents, a destination manifest, a partly filled navigation region, and reduced link inspectors are supplied without final href values or names.
activityDesigns.completionState: The guide passes changed-base, activation, links-only purpose, unique-target, arrival-context, keyboard, and saved-reload checks with faded support.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports the failing destination, purpose, target, or arrival observable and no longer names the correct attribute value or link text.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Faded hints identify the evidence layer and one parallel museum case but omit repair syntax and final wording.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires an independently chosen minimal repair and a changed gallery case before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Complete museum link foundations
interactions.learnerAction: Complete document bases, href attribute boundaries, and heading targets for local, nested, absolute, query, and fragment museum references.
interactions.artifactChange: Five incomplete foundation records become correct.

evidence.observable: Parsed href values, resolved URLs, and destination headings match the museum manifest.
evidence.changedCase: The exhibit document moves beneath a new wing directory.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Trail-guide path or heading structure is copied.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute from museum document relationships.

interactions.title: Build museum destination links
interactions.learnerAction: Author local page, public ticket, email, telephone, accessible-guide file, download, and same-document hyperlinks from the reduced requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven destination types become genuine links.

evidence.observable: Role, focusability, resolved URL, activation, handoff or download trace, and destination identity pass.
evidence.changedCase: Ticket origin, phone format, and guide location change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Strings or pointer clicks substitute for behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect semantics through activation outcome.

interactions.title: Test museum input behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Test every destination by keyboard and pointer while separating focus, activation, navigation, protocol handoff, download preference, context, and history outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: A destination activation matrix is completed.

evidence.observable: Every link behaves predictably without focus-triggered navigation or hidden external side effects.
evidence.changedCase: One href is removed and one target behavior changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One successful click represents all link types.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test each input and consequence separately.

interactions.title: Write museum link purposes
interactions.learnerAction: Write concise destination-specific names for galleries, tickets, contacts, files, and visit details that remain clear in links-only and valid programmatic context.
interactions.artifactChange: Vague museum labels become purposeful.

evidence.observable: Same-purpose names remain consistent and different consequences remain distinguishable.
evidence.changedCase: Two links change from same destination to different destinations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nearby card appearance supplies missing purpose.
interactions.correctionRoute: Audit names outside visual layout.

interactions.title: Test exhibit fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Create and test unique heading targets for exhibit sections while recording URL state, target identity, hierarchy, visibility, focus state, and keyboard continuation.
interactions.artifactChange: The exhibit contents list becomes executable.

evidence.observable: Every fragment arrives at one visible contextual target and reports focus separately.
evidence.changedCase: A banner obscures one heading and a duplicate id is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scroll movement alone passes the fragment check.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect identifier, target, viewport, and focus.

interactions.title: Defend museum navigation
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and explain resolved paths, attributes, heading structure, destination behavior, purpose context, fragment arrival, input parity, and changed museum cases.
interactions.artifactChange: The faded museum navigation checkpoint is stored.

evidence.observable: Reloaded evidence demonstrates all five concepts in a new domain.
evidence.changedCase: A temporary exhibit page and contact link are added independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Working visual navigation lacks causal evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen the weakest link behavior boundary.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A broken campus navigation set gains a causal incident report and minimal repairs for malformed destinations, vague purposes, duplicate fragments, obscured arrivals, and input failures.
activityDesigns.startingState: A campus directory contains a wrong base, malformed href, inconsistent headings, missing href, vague repeated names, duplicate ids, and an obstructed target.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every fault has an earliest cause, observable evidence, minimal repair, regression test, and changed campus case.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies the contradicted URL, DOM, activation, link-list, target, viewport, or focus evidence without revealing the repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints narrow the incident to one evidence layer and then a smaller parallel campus fixture.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires cause classification, minimal repair, preserved unaffected behavior, and a changed fault before the clinic checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Predict foundation faults
interactions.learnerAction: Predict outcomes for a wrong document base, malformed href quoting, duplicated attribute, broken heading rank, and renamed target before opening inspectors.
interactions.artifactChange: Five campus fault predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Each prediction maps to parsed value, resolved URL, target, or hierarchy evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Defects exchange documents and nesting levels.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All failures are called broken links.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify source, base, target, and outline first.

interactions.title: Inspect vague campus purposes
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect repeated learn-more names, programmatic context, destination pairs, section headings, and links-only output to identify ambiguous campus navigation.
interactions.artifactChange: Every ambiguous name receives a purpose diagnosis.

evidence.observable: The audit distinguishes valid context from visual proximity and unrelated headings.
evidence.changedCase: Two labels keep text but swap destinations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual cards or title attributes excuse ambiguity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate links-only and valid context evidence.

interactions.title: Repair campus activation failures
interactions.learnerAction: Repair missing href, wrong relative base, unsafe forced context, malformed contact URL, and broken file destination from focus, URL, request, and outcome evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Five destination behaviors regain minimal correctness.

evidence.observable: Role, focus, resolved URL, activation, destination or handoff, context, and history checks pass.
evidence.changedCase: The file moves and the contact scheme changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Shotgun attribute changes replace causal repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the earliest contradicted behavior.

interactions.title: Repair campus fragment arrival
interactions.learnerAction: Repair vague purpose, duplicate ids, missing target, obscured heading, and broken keyboard continuation while preserving resolved destinations and heading hierarchy.
interactions.artifactChange: The campus fragment system and incident report become correct.

evidence.observable: Purpose, unique target, URL, visibility, focus state, and next keyboard step all pass.
evidence.changedCase: A new fixed obstruction and renamed section are diagnosed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scroll movement or id presence ends diagnosis.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace purpose through arrival layers.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently built tenant resource map connects local guidance, public services, contacts, files, and long-page sections with clear purpose and verified behavior.
activityDesigns.startingState: A stakeholder brief, complete document shell, resource fact packet, public URL contract, sandboxed handoffs, and empty navigation region are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: The saved and reloaded map passes portable path, syntax, hierarchy, activation, purpose, fragment, keyboard, changed-case, and stakeholder-defense checks.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports only the unmet observable and stakeholder consequence; it supplies no destination value, link name, or target identifier.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints are available only on request and point to an evidence layer or smaller parallel resource without giving code.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires independent diagnosis, minimal repair, and a changed tenant-resource case before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Build tenant link foundations
interactions.learnerAction: Organize supplied destinations by document base, author valid href attributes, and preserve content-derived heading hierarchy before building the resource navigation.
interactions.artifactChange: The independent destination and target map is created.

evidence.observable: Every reference, parsed value, document base, and destination heading is justified.
evidence.changedCase: A local guide moves under a language directory.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A prior scenario structure is copied mechanically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive bases and headings from this brief.

interactions.title: Build tenant destination links
interactions.learnerAction: Create genuine local, public, email, telephone, file, download, and fragment links with appropriate same-context defaults and sandboxed external handoffs.
interactions.artifactChange: The tenant resource graph becomes operable.

evidence.observable: Semantics, focus, resolution, activation, destination or handoff, context, and history checks pass.
evidence.changedCase: A public origin, contact number, and file location change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source strings replace executable behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each link through its actual consequence.

interactions.title: Write tenant link purposes
interactions.learnerAction: Write concise names for eligibility, repair requests, emergency contacts, language guides, forms, and sections that remain understandable in valid context.
interactions.artifactChange: Every tenant destination gains purposeful text.

evidence.observable: Links-only and programmatic-context audits distinguish every destination and consequence.
evidence.changedCase: Two forms share a file type but require different action.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Learn more or raw URLs rely on visual cards.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name resource and consequence directly.

interactions.title: Test tenant fragment arrival
interactions.learnerAction: Test each long-page target by keyboard and pointer and record unique identifier, URL fragment, target heading, scroll, visibility, focus state, and next keyboard stop.
interactions.artifactChange: A tenant arrival matrix is stored.

evidence.observable: Every arrival is unique, contextual, visible, predictable, and separately reports focus.
evidence.changedCase: A persistent notice obscures a changed-language target.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Target existence or scroll movement alone passes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect every arrival layer independently.

interactions.title: Run tenant navigation mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Move a local page and file, change a public query, remove href, alter contact schemes, duplicate a target, and make two names ambiguous, then repair fallout.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven adversarial navigation changes are repaired.

evidence.observable: All affected behaviors and purposes update while unrelated navigation remains stable.
evidence.changedCase: Fault order reverses and a target moves between headings.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Happy-path click is accepted as regression proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace every changed dependency and consumer.

interactions.title: Defend tenant resource navigation
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and defend path bases, attributes, hierarchy, destination behavior, context choices, purpose names, fragment arrival, input parity, and mutations.
interactions.artifactChange: The independent tenant navigation checkpoint is stored.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifacts and stakeholder evidence demonstrate all five concepts.
evidence.changedCase: An unseen tenant service and long-page target are added without hints.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A polished map lacks executable stakeholder evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen the weakest observable and changed case.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed mixed-domain retrieval set records path, syntax, heading, destination, purpose, and fragment decisions plus corrected changed cases.
activityDesigns.startingState: Unseen link cards, destination manifests, document bases, links-only outputs, and fragment traces are provided without earlier artifacts or notes.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner retrieves every concept, corrects misconceptions causally, and transfers link behavior to changed contexts after delay.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the missed concept and observable only after commitment and never supplies the final href, name, identifier, or repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Optional retrieval hints point to one evidence layer or smaller parallel item and never reveal the answer.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss routes to a concept-sized correction and different changed retrieval item before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Retrieve URL resolution
interactions.learnerAction: Without notes, resolve six local, public, query, and fragment references from changed document bases and explain each segment consequence.
interactions.artifactChange: Six delayed path answers are recorded.

evidence.observable: Every target follows the exact document or public base and preserves URL components.
evidence.changedCase: Nesting, case, query, and target name all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Project root or remembered example becomes the base.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute one parallel reference from its document.

interactions.title: Correct href syntax
interactions.learnerAction: Correct malformed owner, name, separator, quoting, duplicate, and encoded-value claims for six unfamiliar hyperlink start tags.
interactions.artifactChange: Six syntax claims gain parsed corrections.

evidence.observable: Each repair produces the intended parsed href without altering unrelated attributes.
evidence.changedCase: Values gain spaces, ampersands, and different quoting pressure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source appearance replaces parsed-value evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize one changed start tag.

interactions.title: Predict destination hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Choose heading ranks and target positions for a changed long-page outline before predicting the context available after each fragment arrival.
interactions.artifactChange: A delayed heading-target outline is committed.

evidence.observable: Hierarchy follows content relationships and provides descriptive arrival context with styling removed.
evidence.changedCase: A subsection becomes a peer section and gains children.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Heading rank is chosen for size or fragment convenience.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the content tree before ranks.

interactions.title: Predict destination activation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict semantics, focus, resolved URL, activation, navigation, handoff, download, context, and history for eight mixed link and non-link fixtures.
interactions.artifactChange: Eight activation predictions are locked.

evidence.observable: Every outcome follows the parsed destination and user-agent boundary without hidden side effects.
evidence.changedCase: Href presence, scheme, context, and response type change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Anchor tag or pointer click defines all outcomes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace semantics through actual consequence.

interactions.title: Correct vague link purposes
interactions.learnerAction: Correct eight link names using destination, consequence, links-only output, and valid programmatic context while avoiding redundant or hidden patches.
interactions.artifactChange: Eight delayed purpose cases become defensible.

evidence.observable: Same purposes remain consistent and different destinations remain distinguishable.
evidence.changedCase: Two identical labels acquire different destinations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual card, title attribute, or raw URL supplies purpose.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name destination or useful consequence directly.

interactions.title: Predict fragment arrival
interactions.learnerAction: Predict URL, unique target, hierarchy, scroll, visibility, focus state, and next keyboard position for six fragment and obstruction fixtures.
interactions.artifactChange: Six arrival predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Each layer receives a distinct prediction and browser observable.
evidence.changedCase: A duplicated id, missing target, and fixed obstruction exchange pages.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scrolling is equated with targeting or focus.
interactions.correctionRoute: Predict each arrival layer separately.

interactions.title: Correct mixed navigation failures
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a moved page, missing href, malformed mailto value, ambiguous file link, duplicate target, and obscured heading while preserving unaffected behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Six interleaved retrieval faults are repaired.

evidence.observable: Each minimal repair clears its destination or purpose evidence and survives changed activation.
evidence.changedCase: Fault order and affected destination types change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite hides the recalled mechanism.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair one earliest contradiction at a time.

interactions.title: Explain retained link competence
interactions.learnerAction: Save and explain bases, attributes, headings, destination behavior, purpose context, fragment layers, input parity, minimal corrections, and changed retrieval cases.
interactions.artifactChange: The delayed link-retention checkpoint is stored.

evidence.observable: Saved retrieval evidence demonstrates all five concepts without earlier artifacts.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel mixed navigation set is completed after delay.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Recall confidence substitutes for preserved evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen the weakest concept-sized item.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A no-hint evacuation-support link checkpoint contains independently authored destinations, purpose evidence, fragment traces, repairs, changed cases, and a saved defense.
activityDesigns.startingState: An unseen document set, destination contract, contact and file fixtures, long-page outline, controlled faults, and empty navigation region are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: The candidate independently demonstrates all five concepts through prediction, code, debugging, transfer, persistence, and changed-case defense.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Assessment feedback records only pass or the failed requirement and observable; no canonical href, name, identifier, or repair is disclosed.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No instructional hints, staged clues, example answers, or code completion are available during this checkpoint.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed item closes and records its evidence gap for later relearning plus a different parallel assessment form.

interactions.title: Resolve and parse destinations
interactions.learnerAction: Resolve and parse eight relative, parent, absolute, query, contact, file, and fragment href values from supplied document bases and malformed source.
interactions.artifactChange: Eight scored destination records are stored.

evidence.observable: Parsed values and resolved URLs match exact bases, schemes, components, and intended targets.
evidence.changedCase: Nesting, quoting, query fields, and path case change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Incorrect base, syntax boundary, or URL component.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record path or attribute gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Choose arrival headings
interactions.learnerAction: Choose heading ranks and target positions for an evacuation document outline and defend the context available after four changed fragment arrivals.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored heading-target plan is saved.

evidence.observable: Ranks follow content relationships and preserve descriptive arrival context without styling.
evidence.changedCase: One subsection becomes a peer and another gains children.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rank follows size, order number, or fragment convenience.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record heading-hierarchy gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Author support destinations
interactions.learnerAction: Author local, public, email, telephone, file, download, and fragment hyperlinks with valid attributes and safe deterministic handoff behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven scored destination types become executable.

evidence.observable: Semantics, focus, URL, activation, destination or handoff, context, response, and history pass.
evidence.changedCase: Contact values, public origin, and file location change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source presence or pointer success replaces behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record destination-behavior gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Choose purposeful link names
interactions.learnerAction: Choose and defend concise names for eight support destinations using link-only output, valid programmatic context, consistency, destination, and consequence evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Eight scored purpose decisions are stored.

evidence.observable: Every name distinguishes its useful destination or consequence without visual-only context.
evidence.changedCase: Two links retain text while their destinations diverge.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Purpose relies on nearby layout, title, or raw URL.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record link-purpose gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Build support fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Create unique stable heading targets and a contents list, then record URL, target, hierarchy, scroll, visibility, focus state, and keyboard continuation.
interactions.artifactChange: The scored long-page fragment system is created.

evidence.observable: Every fragment arrives at one intended visible contextual target with separate focus evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A fixed alert obscures one renamed target and must be repaired.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Id presence or scroll movement passes alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record fragment-arrival gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Debug destination failures
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose and minimally repair wrong base, malformed href, missing href, unsafe context, broken contact URL, absent file response, and pointer-only activation assumptions.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven scored destination faults are repaired.

evidence.observable: Each repair clears its earliest behavior failure and preserves unrelated navigation.
evidence.changedCase: Fault order, schemes, and affected documents change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Shotgun attribute edits conceal the cause.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record link-debugging gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Debug purpose and arrival
interactions.learnerAction: Repair vague duplicate names, invalid context assumptions, duplicate identifiers, missing target, obscured heading, and broken keyboard continuation from evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Six scored purpose and fragment faults are repaired.

evidence.observable: Purpose, target cardinality, URL, hierarchy, visibility, focus state, and keyboard behavior pass.
evidence.changedCase: Names and fragment faults exchange destination contexts.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cosmetic rewrite lacks link-list or arrival proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record purpose-fragment gap for relearning.

interactions.title: Submit link behavior checkpoint
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and defend bases, attributes, headings, destinations, activation, purpose context, fragments, visibility, focus state, keyboard parity, repairs, and transfer.
interactions.artifactChange: The complete no-hint link checkpoint is stored.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifacts, traces, repairs, and changed cases prove all five concepts independently.
evidence.changedCase: An unseen support destination and long-page target are added without help.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Submission lacks executable purpose or arrival evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record failed concepts for parallel reassessment.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, responsive, privacy, and duplication reviews are not yet complete.
Learner-facing copy, destination fixtures, sandboxed protocol and download traces, starter documents, canonical answers, executable checks, browser verification, and learner pilots remain unauthored.
